summary: XprtLab Phlebotomist Portal — a mobile-focused medical app for phlebotomists managing hospital and home sample collections. Admin dynamically assigns hospitals; no frontend limits on hospital count. Focused on UI refinement, operational control, and revenue protection without full redesign.
architectural_notes: Hospitals are assigned dynamically by admin — never hardcode or limit hospital count in frontend
Radius and assignment logic are backend/admin-controlled only
Final Amount on Hospital Sample Screen must be auto-calculated and read-only (locked)
Shift must be closed before a new shift can be started
imageRequirements:
## Required Images
- XprtLab official logo — a clean, modern medical diagnostics brand mark with 'XprtLab' wordmark, teal/navy color scheme, suitable for a white or dark header background (xprtlab-logo.dim_320x80.png)

existing_features.title: Remove the 'Built with ❤️ using caffeine.ai' footer/branding text from all screens and layouts.
Add the official XprtLab logo to the header in StaffAppLayout and PatientAppLayout. Slightly reduce the header height for better mobile usability.

Improve global UI spacing, font weights, and contrast across all screens to achieve a professional medical look. Increase font weight for labels and headings; ensure sufficient contrast ratios for text on colored backgrounds.

On the Attendance screen (PhlebotomistAttendancePage / AttendancePage), add a 'Today Summary' section that displays: Total Samples Collected, Cash Collected, UPI Collected, and Pending Amount — computed from the current day's hospital sample records for the logged-in phlebotomist.

On the Attendance screen, rename the 'End Shift' button to 'End Shift & Submit Summary'. When clicked, show a confirmation popup with the message: 'Are you sure you want to end shift? After submission, today's entries cannot be edited.' Only proceed with shift closure if the user confirms.
Enforce that a phlebotomist cannot start a new shift if a previous shift is still open. The 'Start Shift' action must be blocked and display an informative message if an active shift exists.

On the Task Queue page (TaskQueuePage), add a manual 'Refresh' button, display a 'Last Updated' timestamp that updates on each fetch, and enable automatic data refresh every 15 seconds using React Query's refetchInterval. Update the empty state message to: 'No tasks yet. Stay available — new assignments will appear automatically.'

On the Hospital Sample form (AddHospitalSamplePage), make the Hospital field a read-only dropdown populated exclusively from the admin-assigned hospitals list for the logged-in phlebotomist. The frontend must not hardcode any maximum number of hospitals — it must render all hospitals returned by the backend.
On the Hospital Sample form, enforce that the patient mobile number field accepts exactly 10 numeric digits with inline validation. Display an error message if the input is not exactly 10 digits.

On the Hospital Sample form, when a test is selected, display the test's MRP and Offer Price, and auto-calculate the Final Amount. The Final Amount field must be read-only (non-editable) with a lock icon indicator. Price fields must not be manipulable by the user.

On the Hospital Sample form, restrict Payment Mode options to: 'Cash Collected', 'UPI Received', and 'Hospital Credit'. When 'Cash Collected' or 'UPI Received' is selected, auto-fill the Amount Received field with the Final Amount value and make it non-editable. When 'Hospital Credit' is selected, leave Amount Received as zero or empty.

On the Hospital Sample form, add a confirmation dialog before final submission. The dialog must show a summary of the sample entry (patient name, test, final amount, payment mode) and ask: 'Confirm submission? Billing details cannot be edited after saving.'

Redesign the Home Collection Queue page (HomeCollectionQueuePage) so each task card displays: Patient Name, Address, Contact Number, Assigned Tests, Payable Amount, a 'Call' button (opens phone dialer), a 'Start Visit' button, a 'Mark as Collected' button, and a 'Mark as Not Available' button.

Add the following sections to the phlebotomist Profile page: (1) Assigned Hospitals List — showing hospitals assigned by admin, (2) Support / Call Admin — a button that opens a phone dialer to the admin contact number, (3) App Version — static display of the current app version string, (4) Logout confirmation popup — 'Are you sure you want to log out?' with Confirm/Cancel actions before signing out.

Ensure the daily summary (Today Summary) is rendered and visible on the Attendance screen before the 'End Shift & Submit Summary' button is enabled. If no samples have been recorded, show the summary with zero values but still allow shift closure.

new_feature_requests.summary: General UI: Remove caffeine.ai branding, add XprtLab logo, reduce header height, improve spacing/font/contrast
Attendance Screen: Add Today Summary (samples, cash, UPI, pending), rename End Shift button, add confirmation popup, enforce shift closure before new shift
Task Queue: Add manual refresh button, show last updated time, enable 15s auto-refresh, improve empty state message

Hospital Sample Screen: Non-editable hospital dropdown (admin-assigned only, no frontend limit), 10-digit mobile validation, test selection shows MRP/offer price/auto-calculated final amount (locked), payment mode auto-fills amount, prevent price manipulation
Home Collection Screen: Task cards show patient name, address, contact, tests, payable amount, call/start visit/mark collected/mark not available actions
Profile Page: Add assigned hospitals list, support/call admin, app version, logout confirmation popup
Revenue Protection: Lock price editing, prevent manual hospital switching, require save confirmation, enforce daily summary visibility before shift closure

Add or extend backend data model and queries to support: (1) assigning hospitals to a specific phlebotomist (stored as a list per user), (2) querying assigned hospitals for the calling phlebotomist, (3) aggregating today's sample totals (count, cash, UPI, pending/credit) per phlebotomist for the daily summary.
